artifact: IEC 62304 Traceability Matrix (variant A: evidence array per requirement)

rows.requirement_text: GFR category assignment: G1 >=90, G2 60-89, G3a 45-59, G3b 30-44, G4 15-29, G5 <15 mL/min/1.73m^2. AssessRenalFunction's tagged-union return type makes staging a Cockcroft-Gault CrCl value (which is never itself G-staged) a type-level impossibility, not a calling convention a future caller has to remember (Gate 1c Finding 2).

evidence.caveat: Formally proven against the stated specification.
evidence.proof_content_caveat: Definitional proof: the postcondition restates the implementation (ensures is equivalent to the body), so the proof obligation is a tautology discharged by reflexivity. Certified: totality, type-safety, match-exhaustiveness, and the literal boundary structure of the definition. NOT certified: an independent property beyond the definition, or fidelity to the source requirement.

evidence.caveat: Formally proven against the stated specification.
evidence.proof_content_caveat: Property proof: the postcondition is strictly weaker than the implementation (the body implies it but not conversely), so the proof establishes content beyond the definition - a wrong implementation could satisfy the body's shape yet violate it. Still does not certify fidelity of the numbers/modeling to the source.

rows.requirement_text: eGFR is reported rounded to the nearest whole number before staging, shifting the effective continuous G-stage boundaries to 89.5/59.5/44.5/29.5/14.5. The base rounding requirement is KDIGO-sourced; the round-half-up tie-break specifically is a named, uncited design decision - KDIGO states no tie-break rule.

evidence.caveat: Formally proven against the stated specification.
evidence.proof_content_caveat: Property proof: the postcondition is strictly weaker than the implementation (the body implies it but not conversely), so the proof establishes content beyond the definition - a wrong implementation could satisfy the body's shape yet violate it. Still does not certify fidelity of the numbers/modeling to the source.

rows.requirement_text: Formula selection: Cockcroft-Gault CrCl required instead of eGFR for direct-acting oral anticoagulants, nephrotoxic drugs, age >=75, extremes of muscle mass (BMI <18 or >40, strict inequality, verbatim MHRA wording), or narrow-therapeutic-index drugs; eGFR otherwise. On the Cockcroft-Gault branch, CrCl is now computed from raw inputs rather than caller-supplied (Gate 1c Finding 1, closed 2026-07-09 for Cockcroft-Gault only - CKD-EPI eGFR remains caller-supplied, a real Dafny/Z3 expressiveness gap, confirmed empirically, not a choice).

rows.requirement_text: Cockcroft-Gault is unreliable when weight is distorted by obesity or oedema (it is a weight-based formula) - should flag rather than silently compute. Named, sourced (KDIGO), not yet formalized as a Dafny signature.
rows.code_location: none - prose only, not yet formalized
notes: no evidence bound for this requirement

rows.requirement_text: Fail-safe on missing/invalid renal-function data: never default to unadjusted/full dose. Design invariant, same category as dosage.dfy's own fail-safe defaults. Named, not yet formalized as a Dafny signature.
rows.code_location: none - prose only, not yet formalized
notes: no evidence bound for this requirement

rows.requirement_text: Bound composition against dosage.dfy's existing ceiling: the more conservative of the two bounds provably wins, pinned to exactly one of the two inputs, not just bounded above by both (Gate C4 finding and fix, 2026-07-09).

evidence.caveat: Formally proven against the stated specification.
evidence.proof_content_caveat: Property proof: the postcondition is strictly weaker than the implementation (the body implies it but not conversely), so the proof establishes content beyond the definition - a wrong implementation could satisfy the body's shape yet violate it. Still does not certify fidelity of the numbers/modeling to the source.

rows.requirement_text: Reassessment flag on rapidly changing renal function (AKI) - a single value must not be treated as authoritative in that situation. Named, sourced (MHRA), not yet formalized as a Dafny signature.
rows.code_location: none - prose only, not yet formalized
notes: no evidence bound for this requirement

rows.requirement_text: For narrow-therapeutic-index drugs on the eGFR branch, BSA-nonindexed eGFR (mL/min, not mL/min/1.73m^2) may be needed at extremes of body weight. Named, sourced (KDIGO Practice Point 4.2.4), not yet formalized as a Dafny signature.
rows.code_location: none - prose only, not yet formalized
notes: no evidence bound for this requirement

rows.requirement_text: SelectFormula's boolean drug-classification inputs are caller-supplied, not computed inside renal_adjustment.dfy - a deliberate, permanent trust boundary (MHRA's own drug lists are illustrative, not closed or exhaustive; hardcoding them would embed a false-completeness claim inside a formally "proven" artifact). The trust boundary itself is settled and will never be a Dafny proof target. What remains open is only who populates the flags operationally (clinician form / EHR lookup / static list) - a real-world process decision, currently being gathered and deliberately parked as an explicit GAP until that data is in hand. Even once it lands it will be a DECLARED process fact, not a proof (contrast REQ-RENAL-3/4/6/7, which await Dafny formalization). See PHASE1_PLAN.md's "Still open" section.
rows.code_location: none - a process decision, not a Dafny-checkable claim
notes: no evidence bound for this requirement
